evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Playbook openly admits checksum implementation is a simplified, non-functional placeholder ('Paytm sample uses AES + salt; here we create a simplified checksum only for demonstration'). Without the official Checksum/AES+HMAC routine the initiateTransaction call will fail authentication. Endpoint paths (securegw-stage.paytm.in/theia/api/v1/initiateTransaction) match commonly documented form, but core signing is wrong.
evidence.executability: Pydantic v2 imports BaseSettings from pydantic-settings, not pydantic — `from pydantic import BaseSettings` will raise ImportError on modern installs and pydantic-settings is not in pip install. verify_order uses orderId as query param via POST with no Body wrapper. Checksum logic is non-functional. Multiple non-trivial fixes required; no sandbox-infeasibility flags hit.
evidence.completeness: (a) acquisition described; (b) pip and npm install present in code-ish text but not all in ```bash fences (install lines are indented prose, not fenced); (c) no explicit .env template block with KEY=VALUE lines — env vars only mentioned via BaseSettings class fields; (d) backend code present; (e) frontend code present; (f) no runnable test command block. Missing fenced bash and env template are critical gaps.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Multiple official Paytm domain URLs (business.paytm.com/docs, dashboard.paytmpayments.com, paytmpayments.com/docs) placed inline near relevant code sections, plus GitHub official Paytm samples. Some third-party (StackOverflow, pronteff) but official dominates and is anchored near code.

rubric_notes: docs_unavailable=true → long-tail handling applied. Self-admitted non-production checksum is a serious correctness flaw equivalent to a wrong primary call. Pydantic BaseSettings import is a known breaking change in pydantic v2.
